classes.sf-section--collapse: Section variant that removes vertical padding (zero-padding section for adjacent same-color blocks).
classes.sf-section--l: Section variant with large (l) vertical padding.
classes.sf-section--m: Section variant with medium (m) vertical padding.
classes.sf-section--s: Section variant with small (s) vertical padding.
classes.sf-section--xl: Section variant with xl vertical padding.
classes.sf-section--xs: Section variant with xs vertical padding.
classes.sf-section-group: Groups adjacent sf-section elements so their combined padding collapses correctly at the top and bottom edges.
classes.sf-sidebar: Two-column sidebar layout: first child is the sidebar, second child is main content. The sidebar collapses below the breakpoint (--sf-sidebar-min-width). Default sidebar on the left.
classes.sf-sidebar--narrow: Sidebar variant with a narrower sidebar column.
classes.sf-sidebar--right: Sidebar variant with the sidebar on the right (second child becomes the sidebar).
classes.sf-sidebar--wide: Sidebar variant with a wider sidebar column.
classes.sf-stack: Flex column with even vertical spacing between children (--sf-stack-gap). Stacking context for vertical rhythm.
classes.sf-stack--2xl: Stack variant with 2xl gap between children.
classes.sf-stack--center: Stack variant that centers children on the cross axis.
classes.sf-stack--end: Stack variant that aligns children to the cross-axis end (right in LTR).
classes.sf-stack--l: Stack variant with large (l) gap between children.
classes.sf-stack--m: Stack variant with medium (m) gap between children.
classes.sf-stack--s: Stack variant with small (s) gap between children.
classes.sf-stack--stretch: Stack variant that stretches children to fill the full available width.
classes.sf-stack--xl: Stack variant with xl gap between children.
classes.sf-stack--xs: Stack variant with xs gap between children.
classes.sf-subgrid: Passes the parent grid columns through to children via subgrid. Place on a grid item that should align its own children to the outer grid columns.
classes.sf-subgrid-rows: Passes the parent grid rows through to children via subgrid. Place on a grid item that should align its own children to the outer grid rows.
classes.sf-switcher: Flex layout that switches from a horizontal row to a vertical column when the container is narrower than --sf-switcher-threshold.
classes.sf-switcher--no-wrap: Switcher variant that stays horizontal and never wraps (single-line regardless of container width).
classes.sf-switcher--vertical: Switcher variant that starts in the vertical (stacked) direction on all sizes.
classes.sf-aspect: Sets aspect-ratio from a --sf-aspect-ratio scoped token. Override the token inline to get any ratio without a new class.
classes.sf-content-auto: Sets content-visibility: auto on the element. The browser skips rendering off-screen content, improving LCP for long pages.
classes.sf-equal-height: Forces all flex children to equal height (align-items: stretch). Use on a flex row to make cards in a grid share the tallest card's height.
classes.sf-flow: Applies --sf-flow-space as margin-block-start to all direct children except the first. Establishes consistent vertical rhythm in prose-like containers.
classes.sf-line-clamp-2: Clamps text to 2 lines with an ellipsis. Applies -webkit-line-clamp: 2.
classes.sf-line-clamp-3: Clamps text to 3 lines with an ellipsis. Applies -webkit-line-clamp: 3.
classes.sf-line-clamp-N: Clamps text to a custom N lines. Set --sf-line-clamp on the element to control the number of visible lines.
classes.sf-link--reverse: Link color modifier: reverses the default link color to work on dark/inverted backgrounds. Sets color to --sf-color-text--inverse.
classes.sf-link--subtle: Link color modifier: renders the link in the body text color instead of the default action color. Underline still indicates the link on hover.
classes.sf-link-external: Automatically appends an external-link icon (via ::after content) to indicate the link opens in a new tab or goes to an external domain.
classes.sf-no-tap-highlight: Removes the mobile tap highlight color (-webkit-tap-highlight-color: transparent). Use on interactive elements with a custom active state.
classes.sf-not-prose: Opt-out of sf-prose typography styles within a prose block. Apply to an element that should keep default browser or component styles.
classes.sf-overflow-fade: Adds a gradient fade at the inline-end of an overflowing element to hint at hidden content. Use inside sf-reel or any scroll container.
classes.sf-overflow-fade--right: Overflow fade variant: fades the right (inline-end) edge. Explicit alias for the default sf-overflow-fade behaviour.
classes.sf-overflow-fade--left: Overflow fade variant: fades the left (inline-start) edge.
classes.sf-overflow-fade--top: Overflow fade variant: fades the top (block-start) edge.
classes.sf-overflow-fade--bottom: Overflow fade variant: fades the bottom (block-end) edge.
classes.sf-overflow-fade--block: Overflow fade variant: fades both the top and bottom edges simultaneously (block axis).
classes.sf-overflow-fade--inline: Overflow fade variant: fades both the left and right edges simultaneously (inline axis).
classes.sf-prose: Opinionated typographic defaults for long-form content: heading hierarchy, paragraph spacing, blockquote, code, and list styling. Override with sf-not-prose.
classes.sf-scrim: Overlay container with a gradient darkening scrim behind text placed on an image or colored background.
classes.sf-scrim__content: Content area inside an sf-scrim. Positioned above the gradient overlay layer.
classes.sf-scrim--bottom: Scrim variant with the gradient emanating from the bottom edge (bottom-to-top fade).
classes.sf-scrim--full: Scrim variant that covers the entire surface with a uniform translucent overlay.
classes.sf-scrim--top: Scrim variant with the gradient emanating from the top edge (top-to-bottom fade).
classes.sf-scroll-shadow: Adds inset box-shadows at the top and bottom of a scrollable container to indicate hidden overflow above or below.
classes.sf-scroll-snap: Enables CSS scroll-snap-type: x mandatory on a horizontal scroll container. Children should have scroll-snap-align.
classes.sf-surface: Generic semantic surface: applies --sf-surface-color as the background and automatically sets a contrasting text color. Tone variants (--primary, --action, etc.) activate preset palettes.
classes.sf-surface--action: Surface variant using the action color palette (button/CTA primary color).
classes.sf-surface--danger: Surface variant using the danger status palette.
classes.sf-surface--info: Surface variant using the info status palette.
classes.sf-surface--inverse: Surface variant that inverts light/dark, creating an always-dark surface in light mode and always-light in dark mode.
classes.sf-surface--neutral: Surface variant using the neutral palette (muted/gray).
classes.sf-surface--primary: Surface variant using the primary brand color palette.
classes.sf-surface--secondary: Surface variant using the secondary brand color palette.
classes.sf-surface--success: Surface variant using the success status palette.
classes.sf-surface--tertiary: Surface variant using the tertiary brand color palette.
classes.sf-surface--warning: Surface variant using the warning status palette.
classes.sf-tabular-nums: Forces tabular (monospaced) number rendering. Use on price tables, counters, and anywhere numbers need to align vertically across rows.
classes.sf-text-gradient: Applies a brand gradient to inline text. Sets the background to the gradient, clips it to the text, and makes the text color transparent.
classes.sf-text-2xs: Applies the 2xs text-size role: font-size, line-height, font-weight, letter-spacing, and measure (max-inline-size) in one class.
classes.sf-text-xs: Applies the xs text-size role: font-size, line-height, font-weight, letter-spacing, and measure (max-inline-size) in one class.
classes.sf-text-s: Applies the s text-size role: font-size, line-height, font-weight, letter-spacing, and measure (max-inline-size) in one class.
classes.sf-text-m: Applies the m (body) text-size role: font-size, line-height, font-weight, letter-spacing, and measure (max-inline-size) in one class.
classes.sf-text-l: Applies the l text-size role: font-size, line-height, font-weight, letter-spacing, and measure (max-inline-size) in one class.
classes.sf-text-xl: Applies the xl text-size role: font-size, line-height, font-weight, letter-spacing, and measure (max-inline-size) in one class.
classes.sf-text-2xl: Applies the 2xl text-size role: font-size, line-height, font-weight, letter-spacing, and measure (max-inline-size) in one class.
classes.sf-text-3xl: Applies the 3xl text-size role: font-size, line-height, font-weight, letter-spacing, and measure (max-inline-size) in one class.
classes.sf-text-4xl: Applies the 4xl text-size role: font-size, line-height, font-weight, letter-spacing, and measure (max-inline-size) in one class.
classes.sf-text-protect: Wraps text in a translucent dark gradient overlay to ensure legibility when placed over a light or variable background image.
classes.sf-truncate: Single-line text overflow with ellipsis. Equivalent to the classic text-overflow: ellipsis trio (white-space nowrap, overflow hidden, text-overflow ellipsis).
classes.sf-is-active: Active state — applied to the currently interacted-with element (e.g. pressed button, active menu item). Sets --sf-is-active to 1 for calc() branching.
classes.sf-is-busy: Busy state — shows a progress cursor to indicate background processing. Does not mask content; use sf-is-loading when content should be hidden during loading.
classes.sf-is-collapsed: Collapsed state for disclosure widgets — hides the expandable region. Pair with sf-is-expanded or sf-is-open. Components add transitions.
classes.sf-is-current: Current/selected state — marks the active page in navigation or the selected item in a list. Sets --sf-is-current to 1 for calc() branching.
classes.sf-is-danger: Danger status state — applies danger color tokens for destructive actions (e.g. a delete confirmation dialog) and form validation errors.
classes.sf-is-disabled: Disabled state — dims the element (--sf-opacity-disabled), removes pointer events, and sets cursor: not-allowed. Inherited by child elements that re-enable pointer events.
classes.sf-is-draggable: Draggable state — shows a grab cursor to indicate the element can be dragged. Apply before a drag interaction begins.
classes.sf-is-dragging: Active drag state — shows a grabbing cursor while the element is being dragged. Apply during the drag event.
classes.sf-is-drop-target: Drop-target state — applies a visual highlight to indicate a valid drag-and-drop destination. Toggle on drag-over events.
classes.sf-is-empty: Empty state — hides the element when it has no child nodes (:empty). Used for dynamic lists or containers that may render empty.
classes.sf-is-error: Error state — alias for sf-is-danger. Applies danger color tokens to indicate a validation failure or system error. Visual styling is identical to sf-is-invalid.
classes.sf-is-expanded: Expanded state for disclosure widgets — shows the expandable region. Inverse of sf-is-collapsed. Components add transitions.
classes.sf-is-hidden: Hidden state — removes the element from layout and accessibility tree (display: none !important).
classes.sf-is-highlighted: Highlighted state — applies a highlight background. Useful for search result highlighting and keyboard-navigated lists.
classes.sf-is-info: Info status state — applies info color tokens. Use for neutral informational messages and hints.
classes.sf-is-invalid: Invalid state — applies danger color tokens to form fields with validation failures. Identical visual output to sf-is-error; the name signals form-field context.
classes.sf-is-invisible: Invisible state — hides the element visually but keeps it in the layout and accessibility tree (visibility: hidden).
classes.sf-is-loading: Loading state — hides the element's text (color: transparent), removes pointer events, and renders a spinner via ::after. Use for buttons and containers awaiting async results.
classes.sf-is-open: Open/shown state for modals, dropdowns, and drawers — counterpart to sf-is-collapsed/sf-is-hidden. Sets --sf-is-open to 1 for calc() branching.
classes.sf-overlay: Overlay layout helper — absolutely fills its positioned parent (`position: absolute; inset: 0`) for overlays, backdrops, and media scrims.
classes.sf-is-pending: Pending state for optimistic UI — content remains visible but dimmed while an async request is in flight. Differs from sf-is-loading (which hides content entirely).
classes.sf-is-pressed: Pressed/toggled-on state for toggle buttons. Sets --sf-is-pressed to 1 for calc() branching and signals the selected state to components.
classes.sf-is-readonly: Readonly state — removes pointer events and text selection. Use for fields that display data but cannot be edited.
classes.sf-is-selected: Selected state — marks an item as selected in a list, table row, or grid cell. Visual specifics (background, border) are applied by component styles.
classes.sf-is-success: Success status state — applies success color tokens. Use for confirmation messages, completed form steps, and positive feedback.
classes.sf-is-visible: Visible state — forces the element to be visible (visibility: visible). Use to un-hide an element that inherited visibility: hidden from a parent.
classes.sf-is-warning: Warning status state — applies warning color tokens. Use for cautionary messages and non-critical alerts.
classes.sf-color-pulse: Applies a gentle color-pulse animation to draw attention to a dynamic value change (e.g. a counter updating). Scoped to users who have not requested reduced motion.
classes.sf-entrance--fade: Scroll-driven entrance: element fades in as it enters the viewport. Animation tied to scroll progress via animation-timeline.
classes.sf-entrance--fade-down: Scroll-driven entrance: element fades in while moving downward into position.
classes.sf-entrance--fade-left: Scroll-driven entrance: element fades in while moving from the right toward the left.
classes.sf-entrance--fade-right: Scroll-driven entrance: element fades in while moving from the left toward the right.
classes.sf-entrance--fade-up: Scroll-driven entrance: element fades in while moving upward into position.
classes.sf-entrance--scale-up: Scroll-driven entrance: element fades in while scaling up from a slightly smaller size.
classes.sf-fade-in: One-shot fade-in animation (opacity 0 → 1). Scoped to no-preference so it stops automatically for reduced-motion users.
classes.sf-fade-out: One-shot fade-out animation (opacity 1 → 0). Scoped to no-preference.
classes.sf-scale-down: One-shot scale-down animation (element shrinks from full size). Scoped to no-preference.
classes.sf-scale-up: One-shot scale-up animation (element grows from smaller size). Scoped to no-preference.
classes.sf-slide-in-down: One-shot slide-in-from-above animation. Scoped to no-preference.
classes.sf-slide-in-left: One-shot slide-in-from-right animation (slides to the left). Scoped to no-preference.
classes.sf-slide-in-right: One-shot slide-in-from-left animation (slides to the right). Scoped to no-preference.
classes.sf-slide-in-up: One-shot slide-in-from-below animation. Scoped to no-preference.
classes.no-print: Hides the element in @media print. Use on sidebars, navigation bars, and decorative elements that should not appear on paper.
classes.print-color-exact: Forces exact color rendering in print (print-color-adjust: exact). Use on charts and colored badges that must preserve their fill colors in print.
classes.print-no-color: Strips color information in print (print-color-adjust: economy). Defers to the printer's ink-saving mode for backgrounds and non-critical color fills.
classes.sf-theme-transition: Opt-in animated light/dark theme crossfade. Apply to <html> (or a subtree) so color tokens transition smoothly when [data-theme] changes. Duration controlled by --sf-theme-transition-duration.
classes.sf-is-skeleton: Skeleton loading placeholder state. Applies a shimmer animation over the element to indicate that content is loading. Works on both img and non-media elements.
classes.sf-is-valid: Valid state — applies success color tokens to form fields that have passed validation. Visual styling is identical to sf-is-success; the name signals form-field context.
classes.print-only: Hides the element on screen (display: none) but shows it in @media print. Use for print-only headers, footers, and supplementary content not needed on screen.
classes.theme-transition: Internal helper class used by the sf-theme-transition macro. Applies per-property transitions to registered color tokens during light/dark mode switches.
classes.sf-bg-layer: Background layer — absolutely-positioned cover media that sits behind a parent's content. The parent is auto-promoted to a positioned, isolated stacking context via :has(), so .sf-bg-layer composes natively under .sf-scrim (background → scrim gradient → content). Works as a bare or as a wrapper with a nested > img / > video / > picture.
